notes: Widget enumeration uses page-level /Annots traversal (not reader.get_fields()) so that names include the full parent path (topmostSubform[0].PageN[0]...). reader.get_fields() also returns non-terminal group containers that do NOT have a widget annotation; we skip those and count only terminal widgets.

The IRS-hosted 2025 Form 1040 PDF (title "2025 Form 1040") was downloaded on 2026-04-11. Although this research wave was scoped to TY2024, the IRS has already published the TY2025 PDF at the canonical url and we mapped that instead — Layer 1 Form1040Fields was already written against the TY2024 layout and is mostly identical, but TY2025 renumbers line 12 into 12a..12e and splits line 13 into 13a/13b and line 27 into 27a/b/c. See the methodology doc and per-field `note` entries for the renumbering.

filing_status is a checkbox *group* — Layer 1 stores the string status and filling the PDF requires toggling exactly one of the five entries under `filing_status_checkboxes`. For MFJ/QSS/Single/HOH each row has TWO widget annotations (Checkbox_ReadOrder[0].c1_8[i] and .Page1[0].c1_8[i]) — these are mirrors of the same logical field; toggling either one in the PDF viewer sets both.

taxpayer_name and spouse_name are Layer 1 *strings*. The TY2025 PDF has separate widgets for first+middle-initial and last name. We map the first-name widget only; future Schedule A/B/C/SE agents should extend Layer 1 to carry the name split if signature overlay is needed.
line_11 carries a page-1 widget (f1_75 — line 11a AGI) AND a page-2 widget (f2_01 — line 11b brought-forward AGI). See computed_copies.

For every unmapped widget, a short reason describes *why* it is not in Form1040Fields. Most unmapped widgets fall into one of 5 categories: header identity (name/SSN/address), dependents table (16 rows × 4 cols), filing-status / Sched-B / digital-asset checkboxes, income sublines (1b-1i, 3c, 4c, 5c, 6c, 6d, 7b), and page-2 footer (refund banking, signatures, designee, preparer).

filing_status.note: One of five mutually-exclusive filing-status checkboxes. See `filing_status_checkboxes` for the exact widget-per-status mapping. The Layer 1 filing_status string selects exactly one.

taxpayer_name.note: Taxpayer first name + middle initial. Layer 1 concatenates first+last into one string; filling requires splitting and also writing the last name into the companion widget f1_02.

spouse_name.note: Spouse first name + middle initial. The companion last-name widget on this row is inferred to also live in the identity block; see unmapped_widgets.

line_11_adjusted_gross_income.note: Line 11a on page 1. The same value must also be copied into line 11b (f2_01) at the top of page 2 — see `computed_copies`.

line_12_standard_or_itemized_deduction.note: TY2025 Form 1040 renumbered this to line 12e. Layer 1 uses the historical `line_12_` name; the widget is line 12e.

line_13_qbi_deduction.note: TY2025 line 13a (QBI). Companion line 13b (additional deductions from Schedule 1-A, new OBBBA) is widget f2_04, tracked in unmapped_widgets until Layer 1 is extended.

line_14_sum_12_13.note: TY2025 line 14 = 12e + 13a + 13b. Layer 1 computes 12+13 only — caller must ensure 13b is accounted for before overlaying.

line_27_earned_income_credit.note: TY2025 subline 27a. The 27a/b/c split (27a=EIC amount, 27b=clergy SE checkbox, 27c=opt-out checkbox) is new for 2025. Layer 1 carries only the amount.

line_11_adjusted_gross_income.note: Line 11b on page 2 — must be written to the same value as line 11a (f1_75) on page 1.
